edges.description: Loss of the heme a cofactor yields a catalytically inactive enzyme.
edges.source: COX15 Loss and Defective Heme A Biosynthesis
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy failure in cardiomyocytes drives early-onset hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Impaired Complex IV electron transfer and oxidative ATP synthesis shift metabolism toward glycolytic lactate production, producing lactic acidosis in COX deficiency.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

nodes.description: Biallelic COX15 variants disrupt heme A synthase, the final step of heme A biosynthesis, depriving COX of its heme a prosthetic group and preventing assembly of a functional holoenzyme.
nodes.id: COX15 Loss and Defective Heme A Biosynthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in COX15 produce a defect in the mitochondrial heme biosynthetic pathway, causing early-onset fatal hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
evidence.snippet: COX15, a protein involved in the synthesis of heme A, the heme prosthetic group for COX, can functionally complement the isolated COX deficiency

nodes.description: Early-onset fatal hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, the classic COX15 phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in COX15 produce a defect in the mitochondrial heme biosynthetic pathway, causing early-onset fatal hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in COX15 produce a defect in the mitochondrial heme biosynthetic pathway, causing early-onset fatal hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

nodes.description: Loss of functional COX blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis, with greatest impact on cardiac muscle.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.

nodes.description: Elevated lactate from impaired oxidative metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Cytochrome c oxidase deficiency: patients and animal models.
evidence.snippet: Human diseases associated with COX deficiency including encephalomyopathies, Leigh syndrome, hypertrophic cardiomyopathies, and fatal lactic acidosis are caused by mutations in COX subunits or assembly factors.
